bulletins.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: An unfavourable avalanche situation will prevail. Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the early morning as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a rapid increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Most and wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes below approximately 2500 m.
As the penetration by moisture increases the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses. More frequent wet slab avalanches are to be expected in the afternoon. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes between approximately 2200 and 2600 m. Especially here wet avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach large size. This also applies in isolated cases in highly frequented off-piste terrain. In steep gullies the avalanches can reach areas without any snow cover.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted. For this reason the snowpack will only just freeze. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2800 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
West, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are quite favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Individual natural avalanches are possible as the day progresses. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
The no longer entirely fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases in particular on very steep shady slopes in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are generally favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs at elevated altitudes. In particular, however, the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Above approximately 2500 m: As a consequence of new snow and northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last three days adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers and reach large size in particular on very steep shady slopes.

In particular on very steep grassy slopes and on rocky slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses as the moisture increases. Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack.
Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m. In the last two days above approximately 2500 m numerous small and medium-sized avalanches were observed. Several occasionally large powder avalanches have been released at high altitude.

Shady slopes as well as high altitudes: Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

A clear night: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.
Below approximately 2500 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be mild. A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation the snow drift accumulations will stabilise during the next few days.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Individual natural avalanches are possible as the day progresses. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are generally favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2800 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. On steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases. Wind slabs are mostly small.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m snow has fallen since Monday over a wide area. Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2800 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights:
On Thursday it will be sunny. The weather will be mild.
A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet avalanches will increase later.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches within the current danger level. Individual natural avalanches are possible as the day progresses. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Especially on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released by people, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The backcountry touring conditions in the morning at elevated altitudes are generally favourable. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last three days adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. These can be released, in particular by large loads and reach medium size. This applies in particular on very steep slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In particular on very steep slopes small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the existence of a weak snowack. Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m. Since Tuesday above approximately 2500 m several small and medium-sized avalanches were observed.

A clear night: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be mild.
A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. In the event of an overcast night this applies.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
If there is cloud cover at night combined with warm air, the avalanche risk will already be 2 (moderate) by the morning because The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze. And so conditions in the mid-mountain range will already be somewhat unfavourable.
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will quickly increase. In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are to be expected.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate southeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed on Monday. They have formed at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack is hardly frozen at all. 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Monday above approximately 2300 m. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to settling of the snowpack. As the moisture increases more moist and wet avalanches are possible.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released, especially by large additional loads, at high altitude. This applies in particular on very steep slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

In particular on very steep slopes small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Naturally triggered avalanches and penetration of the old snow cover indicate the existence of a weak snowack. Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 5 to 30 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2100 m. The sleet gave rise on Tuesday to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning. This applies in particular on steep east, south and west facing slopes below approximately 2700 m, as well as on north facing slopes below approximately 2400 m.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be mild.
A clear night will be followed by favourable conditions generally, but the danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase later.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring.
The surface of the snowpack will freeze, but a strong crust will not form and will already soften in the late morning.
If there is cloud cover at night combined with warm air, the avalanche risk will already be 2 (moderate) by the morning because The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night. And so conditions in the mid-mountain range will already be somewhat unfavourable.
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase. In starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate southeasterly wind, mostly small wind slabs formed on Monday. They have formed at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Colder than expected: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust. New snow and wind slabs are lying on a wet old snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1900 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more moist and wet avalanches are possible. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to settling of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: More wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights: On Thursday it will be sunny. The weather will be mild.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow requires caution. As a consequence of warming and solar radiation a sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities up to 20 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2600 m. Up to 2300 m rain has fallen in some localities.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable in the fog. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2500 m. From starting zones that have retained some snow in particular the wet avalanches can reach large size. The new snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above approximately 2500 m. In particular on very steep slopes and at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible. Bases of rock walls are especially dangerous.
As a consequence of new snow and wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed especially above approximately 2500 m. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous.
In some localities increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The sleet gave rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2200 m. The new snow is loosely bonded above approximately 2700 m. The wind slabs are poorly bonded with the old snowpack above approximately 2700 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather conditions gave rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover and snowpack depth vary greatly depending on aspect and elevation; in general, the snowpack is saturated on all aspects and is becoming increasingly thin. At low and intermediate altitudes a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place gliding avalanches can reach intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. Great caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment: The rain gave rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
Warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.
Above approximately 2400 m snow fell in the last few days over a wide area.
As a consequence of wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
Warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack is hardly frozen at all and will already be soft in the early morning.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. In very isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
